item.title: Biography
item.description: Biography Masaki Kohana is an assistant professor at Ibaraki University from Feb 2017. He was an assistant professor at Department of Computer and Information Science, Seikei Univeristy from Apr 2013 to Jan 2017. His research interests includes web-based systems, parallel and distributed systems, and text mining. He is a member of ACM, IEEE Computer Society, IEICE, and IPSJ. Research Interests Web System Parallel and Distributed System Text Mining Education Ph.

item.title: Activity
item.description: Society International Workshop on Web Services and Social Media Co-Chair, 2015-2016 Program Committee Member, 2013-2014 The 17th International Conference on Network-Based Information Systems (NBiS2014) Program Committee Member IEICE ISS Cyberworlds (Type-2 Technical Group) (Link:Japanese) , Secretary, 2014-present Special Section on Cyberworlds, IEICE Transactions on Information & Systems, Editing Secretary, 2016 Award IDRユーザフォーラム2017 企業賞「Sansan株式会社」 Best Paper Award, The 18th International Conference on Network-Based Information Systems (NBiS2015) Workshop, 2015.

item.title: Teaching
item.description: Current Ibaraki University Master Data Analysis, 2017- Mathematical Analysis, BigData Strategy, 2018- Bachelor Information Skill, 2018- Past Seikei University Advanced Programming, 2015-2016 Advanced Programming Experiment, 2013-2016 Java Programming, 2013-2015 Java Programming Experiment, 2013-2015 Fundamental Programming Experiment, 2012-2016 Fundamental Programming, 2013-2014 Regular Programming Experiment, 2012

item.title: Contact
item.description: Address Building S1 Room 812, Ibaraki University Nakanarusawa 4-12-1, Hitachi, Ibaraki, Japan, 316-8511 E-mail masaki.kohana.gopher [at] vc.ibaraki.ac.jp
